title: Cycle Coherence — Every Loop Returns Home
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: A constraint system is consistent if and only if parallel transport around every cycle in the constraint graph returns the identity transformation
content.proof: Parallel transport along constraint edges defines a connection on the constraint graph. If the connection has zero curvature (holonomy = identity for all cycles), the constraint system is globally consistent. This is Stokes' theorem on a graph: the sum of local violations around a cycle equals the total violation. Zero holonomy = zero total violation = global consistency.
content.benchmark: Cycle coherence check (1000 constraints): 150μs. Full graph verification: O(V + E) for tree-based verification, O(V·C) for full cycle enumeration where C = cycle count.
content.caveat: Full cycle enumeration is exponential. Practical approach: check spanning tree + fundamental cycles. This covers all independent cycles.
